Version No. 011
Educational Grants Act 1973
Act No. 8495/1973
Version incorporating amendments as at 1 July 1997
An Act to make Provision with respect to Financial Aid for
certain Schools.
BE IT ENACTED by the Queen's Most Excellent Majesty by
and with the advice and consent of the Legislative Council
and the Legislative Assembly of Victoria in this present
Parliament assembled and by the authority of the same as
follows (that is to say):
1. Short title and commencement
(1) This Act shall be cited as the Educational Grants
Act 1973.
(2) This Act shall come into operation on the 1st day
of January, 1974.
2. Definitions
In this Act, unless inconsistent with the context or
subject-matter—
"non-government school" means a school, other
than a school conducted by the State at
which full-time education is provided for
pupils in primary or secondary classes, at
which primary education or secondary
education is, or both are, provided, but does
not include a school conducted for the profit,
direct or indirect, of an individual or
individuals;
1

stnemucoD
yratnemailraP
dna
noitalsigeL
nairotciV
Educational Grants Act 1973
s. 2
Act No. 8495/1973
"primary pupil" means a child who is included
on the school roll of a non-government
school on the schools census date and who is
receiving full-time education provided for
pupils at that school being education of a
kind similar to that provided for pupils in
primary classes in schools conducted by the
State;
"qualified accountant" means—
(a) a person who is registered as a
company auditor;
(b) a member of the Institute of Chartered
Accountants in Australia or of the
Australian Society of Accountants; or
(c) a person approved by the Minister as a
person competent to furnish such
statements;
"recurrent expenditure", in relation to a non-
government school, means expenditure
incurred for purposes related to the provision
of primary education or secondary education
at the school;
"school authority", in relation to a non-
government school, means the person or
body conducting the school;
"secondary pupil" means a child who is included
on the school roll of a non-government
school on the schools census date and who is
receiving full-time education provided for
pupils of that school being education of a
kind similar to that provided for pupils in
secondary classes in schools conducted by
the State;
2

stnemucoD
yratnemailraP
dna
noitalsigeL
nairotciV
Educational Grants Act 1973
s. 3
Act No. 8495/1973
"schools census date" means the date in the year
as at which the Commonwealth Statistician
compiles statistics in relation to the numbers
of pupils in schools in this State;
"year" means a calendar year ending on the 31st S. 2 def. of
"year"
day of December, 1983.
amended by
Nos 8632 s. 2,
8790 s. 2, 8930
s. 2, 9062 s. 2,
9220 s. 2, 9352
s. 2, 9497 s. 2,
9661 s. 2, 9830
s. 2.
3. Capitation grants
(1) Subject to this section the Minister may pay S. 3(1)
amended by
capitation grants to the school authority of a non-
No. 31/1994
government school. s. 3(Sch. 1
item 22).
(2) Any payment made under sub-section (1) shall be
subject to the following conditions:
(a) The school authority will ensure that
amounts equal to the total of all payments
made by the Minister to the school authority
in respect of the school in accordance with
this section are applied in that year or not
later than three months after the end of that
year for the purposes of recurrent
expenditure in respect of the school;
(b) The school authority will cause to be
furnished to the Minister not later than six
months after the end of the year in which the
payment is made a statement in writing
signed by a qualified accountant to the effect
that he has satisfied himself that the
condition referred to in the last preceding
sub-paragraph has been complied with and
3

stnemucoD
yratnemailraP
dna
noitalsigeL
nairotciV
Educational Grants Act 1973
s. 3
Act No. 8495/1973
specifying the amount or amounts paid to the
school authority in respect of that year;
(c) The school authority will cause to be
furnished to the Minister, not later than six
months after the end of the year in which the
payment is made a statement in writing
specifying the total income derived and the
total expenditure incurred by the school
authority in respect of primary and
secondary education at that school in respect
of that year and also specifying in respect of
each class of income or class of expenditure
determined by the Minister the total amount
of income of that class derived or the total
amount of expenditure of that class incurred
by the school authority in respect of primary
and secondary education at that school in
respect of that year;
(d) If the total of the grants paid under this
section to a school authority in respect of the
year exceeds the amount payable in
accordance with the provisions of this
section, the school authority will repay to the
Minister the amount of the excess.
(3) Where the Minister is satisfied that the school
authority has failed to fulfil a condition to which
the payment is made subject by this Act the
Minister may direct that an amount equal to the
whole or a part of a payment made to the school
authority under this section shall be deducted from
further amounts becoming payable to the school
authority under this Act or under any
corresponding subsequent enactment.
(4) Payments to a school authority under this section
may be made at such times and in such
instalments as the Minister determines.
4

of—
(a) $374 in respect of each primary pupil; S. 3(5)(a)

4. Regulations
The Governor in Council may make regulations
for or with respect to prescribing any matter or
thing that is authorized or required to be
prescribed and that is convenient to be prescribed
to enable effect to be given to this Act.
5. Repeal
(1) The Educational Grants Act 1972 is hereby
repealed.
(2) The Educational Grants Act 1972
notwithstanding its repeal shall continue to have
the same operation and effect in and in relation to
the year 1973 as it would have had if it had not
been repealed.
5
